idea.title: Loom Weaver
idea.hook: Weave colored threads row by row to reveal today's hidden tapestry — earlier rows hide under later ones.
idea.prototype: 10-wide warp, 12 rows. Each turn pick a color (5 available) and a 'skip pattern' (which warp threads it goes over/under, chosen from 4 stencils per turn). Target tapestry shown faintly. Threads ABOVE physically occlude rows below at matching cells — so order matters: laying a thread early lets later rows hide its mistakes. Thread budget: 12 wefts. Score = % of visible cells matching target. Daily seed picks target + stencil set.
idea.mutation_of: r/WebGames Pixolve (daily pixel-art nonogram) + r/WebGames Defino. Mutates nonogram from 'fill cells' into 'lay ordered layers'
idea.why_novel: Daily puzzle games on portals never use occlusion-by-stacking. Each row is partly painted-over by future rows, so you plan visibility from the FINAL state backward.
idea.lose_condition: 12 wefts placed but match % below daily threshold (e.g. 80%)

sources.title: Loom Weaver
sources.snippet: Entry #5 — Loom Weaver: weave colored threads row by row to reveal today's hidden tapestry.

synthesizer_brief: Layered-tapestry daily puzzle: 5 colors × 4 stencils × 12 weft budget. Occlusion ordering as the novel pressure axis. Daily seed picks target + stencil set.

alternatives.title: Mirror Duelist
alternatives.hook: Mirror an AI fencer's stance combos using a 4-button parry chord
alternatives.why_novel: Mirror-input reads the AI's silhouette and you commit a chord — no twitch, all read

alternatives.title: Pendulum Sniper
alternatives.hook: Time shots against a swinging pendulum that occludes targets
alternatives.why_novel: Occlusion-timing — the pendulum hides AND reveals different targets per phase

alternatives.title: Mason Hammer
alternatives.hook: Chip stone blocks into the daily silhouette using shaped chisels
alternatives.why_novel: Subtractive shape matching against a daily target silhouette with a chisel budget
